sectionOrderRules: Hero must state the editorial offering or media focus and include a primary CTA tied to subscription or latest content.
Featured-content must appear above the fold or immediately after hero and highlight real current items, not placeholder stories.
Newsletter-signup should appear before the final contact CTA.
Editorial-trust should reference concrete trust signals such as publication rhythm, editorial identity or named team members when available.
Sections must avoid repeating the same headline story or CTA wording multiple times.
News-intro must explain what kind of updates, reports or broadcasts are published here.
Content-feed must prioritize clarity and chronology or editorial selection over decorative layout.
If featured-content is present it should lead into, not duplicate, the main feed.
Newsletter-signup should be placed after meaningful content context, not before the visitor understands the offer.
Program-intro must explain the types of programs, formats or coverage areas offered.
Program-list items must be concrete and distinguishable, for example by theme, audience or publication format.
If schedule-note is used it must only describe actual publication or broadcast rhythm when known.
Contact-cta should invite tips, editorial contact or collaboration enquiries without overstating availability.
About-story must describe the editorial mission, focus or history in specific terms.
Team should show real people or roles when available and be omitted rather than filled with generic placeholders.
Editorial-principles should cover how the outlet works, such as sourcing, corrections or scope, without making unsupported claims.
Trust-proof may reinforce but must not duplicate the same team or mission content.
Newsletter-intro must clearly state what subscribers receive and the expected sending rhythm when known.
Newsletter-signup must be the primary action on the page.
Newsletter-benefits should stay specific to the publication, such as alerts, curated analysis or weekly summaries, and avoid vague marketing copy.
If trust-proof is used it should support the value of subscribing, for example by referencing editorial focus or consistency.
Contact-info must provide real contact channels relevant to editorial operations, such as email and other available channels.
Contact-form should clarify the type of enquiries it is for, for example tips, questions or general contact.
Map-embed should only be shown when a public visiting address is actually relevant.
The page should not imply guaranteed response times unless supplied by source data.
